message:
Add Pro CTA and Login links to navigation

- Add Login link (secondary style) and Go Pro button (primary with Crown icon)
- Desktop: Shows links before basket icon, visible on lg+ screens
- Mobile: Adds links in dedicated section at bottom of mobile menu
- Uses NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL env var with fallback to pro.jeffreysprompts.com
- Mobile menu max-height increased to accommodate new links

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-skwl","title":"Public site: add Pro CTA + Login in Nav","description":"## Goal
Expose a clear, subtle path to JeffreysPrompts Pro without disrupting the free-first UX. Add a primary CTA for upgrading and a secondary Login link for existing Pro users.

## Where
- Component: `apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx`
- Ensure both **desktop** and **mobile** navs get the links.
- The nav is rendered in `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`.

## UX Requirements
- **Free-first**: CTA should be present but not dominate the main nav.
- **Desktop**:
  - Add a text link: `Login` (secondary style).
  - Add a button-like link: `Go Pro` or `Upgrade` (primary accent).
- **Mobile**:
  - Include the same links inside the mobile sheet/menu.
  - `Go Pro` should still read as primary.

## Copy + Destinations
- Base URL: `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`
- CTA: `Go Pro` -> `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`
- Login: `Login` -> `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com/login`
- If we later change routes, use a single source of truth constant (see below).

## Implementation Notes
- Add a public env var, e.g. `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` (default to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`).
- In `apps/web/src/lib/env.ts` (or equivalent), add schema + runtime mapping.
- In `Nav.tsx`, build URLs from `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL`:
  - CTA: `${PRO_URL}/`
  - Login: `${PRO_URL}/login`
- Use existing button/link styles (prefer shadcn Button or existing nav button classes).

## Edge Cases
- If the env var is missing, fallback to hardcoded `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`.
- Keep `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on external links; `target="_blank"` only if the nav uses it elsewhere (otherwise same-tab).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Desktop + mobile nav show both `Go Pro` and `Login`.
- Links route to the Pro site endpoints above.
- Styling feels consistent with current nav and maintains free-first tone.
- No layout regression on mobile (no wrapping or overflow).

+{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-skwl","title":"Public site: add Pro CTA + Login in Nav","description":"## Goal
Expose a clear, subtle path to JeffreysPrompts Pro without disrupting the free-first UX. Add a primary CTA for upgrading and a secondary Login link for existing Pro users.

## Where
- Component: `apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx`
- Ensure both **desktop** and **mobile** navs get the links.
- The nav is rendered in `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`.

## UX Requirements
- **Free-first**: CTA should be present but not dominate the main nav.
- **Desktop**:
  - Add a text link: `Login` (secondary style).
  - Add a button-like link: `Go Pro` or `Upgrade` (primary accent).
- **Mobile**:
  - Include the same links inside the mobile sheet/menu.
  - `Go Pro` should still read as primary.

## Copy + Destinations
- Base URL: `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`
- CTA: `Go Pro` -> `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`
- Login: `Login` -> `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com/login`
- If we later change routes, use a single source of truth constant (see below).

## Implementation Notes
- Add a public env var, e.g. `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL` (default to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`).
- In `apps/web/src/lib/env.ts` (or equivalent), add schema + runtime mapping.
- In `Nav.tsx`, build URLs from `NEXT_PUBLIC_PRO_URL`:
  - CTA: `${PRO_URL}/`
  - Login: `${PRO_URL}/login`
- Use existing button/link styles (prefer shadcn Button or existing nav button classes).

## Edge Cases
- If the env var is missing, fallback to hardcoded `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`.
- Keep `rel="noopener noreferrer"` on external links; `target="_blank"` only if the nav uses it elsewhere (otherwise same-tab).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Desktop + mobile nav show both `Go Pro` and `Login`.
- Links route to the Pro site endpoints above.
- Styling feels consistent with current nav and maintains free-first tone.
- No layout regression on mobile (no wrapping or overflow).

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-sqe","title":"Implement transcript processor (lib/transcript/processor.ts)","description":"Part 10.4 of PLAN. processTranscript(rawJsonl) parses JSONL, extracts messages/toolCalls/thinking, auto-detects sections via heuristics (ultrathink = deep thinking, clusters of Edit = implementation, etc), calculates stats (userMessages, assistantMessages, toolCalls, filesEdited, linesWritten, tokensUsed). Returns ProcessedTranscript for display.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:44:50.293889927-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:37:50.793281326-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:37:50.793281326-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented transcript processor in apps/web/src/lib/transcript/processor.ts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-sqe","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:33.823124595-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t1ok","title":"Implement jfp logout and jfp whoami commands","description":"Implement the logout and whoami commands for CLI auth management. whoami shows current user email, tier, and token expiry. logout clears local credentials and optionally revokes token on server. See epic for full context.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:11:03.685841575-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:11:03.685841575-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t1ok","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-tyb1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:13:36.509432351-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t2a","title":"Create install script routes (/install.sh, /install-cli.sh) -- Details: /install.sh - bash script installs all skills via HEREDOC. /install-cli.sh - downloads jfp binary, verifies SHA256, installs to ~/.local/bin. Platform detection (linux/darwin/arm64/x64).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:27:19.022306631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:40:01.202780815-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:40:01.202780815-05:00","close_reason":"Created install script routes: /install.sh (generates HEREDOC-based skill installer using generateInstallScript from core package, supports ?featured=true param), /install-cli.sh (platform detection for linux/darwin x64/arm64/windows, SHA256 verification, installs to ~/.local/bin). Both routes tested and working. Build passes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-t2a","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wum","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:39:16.005807111-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
